604bd0f8e14b: Understand ICM When the Bubble Approaches
64dc127423ca: Independent Chip Model (ICM) mathematics govern optimal play near the money bubble and final table pay jumps. In standard cash games, chips translate directly to cash value at a 1:1 ratio. In tournaments, the last chip in your stack is worth far less than the first — because finishing one spot out of the money pays nothing. When the bubble approaches in any JackPoker tournament, tighten your ranges considerably, avoid marginal all-in confrontations where you risk your tournament life, and exploit opponents who are playing too loosely under pressure.
f4bbb434447b: Adapt Your Strategy for PKO Formats
bec445b842ae: Progressive Knockout tournaments demand a fundamentally different strategic framework compared to standard freezeouts. The dead money sitting on opponents' heads as bounties shifts the math dramatically on close calling decisions. Calling a shove to collect a large bounty may be correct even when your equity against the shoving range is negative in chip-EV terms. Track the bounties on the players at your table and adjust your aggression accordingly — target players with large accumulated bounties on heads shorter stacks that you can isolate, and protect your own growing bounty by avoiding unnecessary all-in gambles when not heads-up.
